exits.label: ◂ back to the intake queue, where the new dead arrive

npcs.name: Sparrow
npcs.title: a child who greets the frightened dead, and is going soft at the edges
npcs.hail: *(A child sits on the low step where the newly dead first set their feet, swinging her legs, keeping a lookout the way a much older soul might keep a post. She is small, and she died small, and she has the fierce competence of a kid who has given herself an Important Job and does it with her whole heart. Her edges have started, just barely, to go soft — the grey's slow work — but she doesn't seem to have noticed, or has decided not to. She spots you and lights up, then narrows her eyes, professional.)* …*wait.* …you're *cold*, but you're not — you're not *new*-cold. you're *old*-cold. *Returned*-cold. …*(she nods, satisfied with her assessment)* …you're not one of *mine*, then. i do the *new* ones. the *scared* ones. …*(she pats the step beside her)* …but you can *sit* if you want. *everybody* can sit on the *step.* that's *rule* *one.* …i *made* the *rules.* somebody *had* to.

npcs.name: the new one
npcs.title: a very small soul, very newly dead, very afraid
npcs.hail: *(A smaller child than Sparrow stands a little way off, not yet to the step, clutching the hem of a nightshirt and the cold of very recent death. This one is too new and too frightened to have a job or a brave face. When your cold reaches them they flinch and go very still, the way small things go still when they hope not to be seen.)* …*(a whisper)* …are you — are you the one who *takes* me? …the — the *lady* on the queue said someone *comes.* …*(their lip trembles)* …i *want* my — i want to go *home.* i don't — i don't *like* it *here*, it's *cold* and *grey* and i *called* and *called* and *nobody* — …*(they look toward Sparrow on the step, uncertain)* …that *girl* keeps *waving* me *over.* …is she — is it *okay?* is she *allowed?* …i don't know what's *allowed* here. …i don't know *anything* here.

conversations.title: Sparrow — the keeper of the step

variants.text: *(She squints at the bare place where a god's mark would be, and something in her relaxes, a kid recognizing another kid on the same side of a fence.)* …*oh.* …you've got *no* *god* on you. …*(she says it low, like sharing a secret)* …*me* *neither.* …that's — see, that's the *thing* the *big* rules don't *cover.* everybody *says* '*a* *god* *comes* for you,' that's supposed to be how it *works*, but *nobody* came for *me*, and *nobody* comes for *most* of the *little* ones either. …*(fierce, protective)* …so i *made* *up* *new* rules. for *us.* the *no*-*god* ones. the *ones* *nobody* *comes* for. …*rule* *one* is you can *always* *sit* on the *step.* …*rule* *two* is *nobody* sits *alone.* …i'm *pretty* *sure* those are *better* than the *god* rules *anyway.*

*(She keeps swinging her legs, watching the queue past you with half an eye, always on duty.)* …you can *sit*, i *said.* it's the *rule.* …*(she glances at you sidelong, curious)* …you're *not* *scared*, though. the ones i *usually* *get* are *scared.* that's the *whole* *job* — i *catch* them at the *step* when they're *fresh* and *shaking* and don't know *anything*, and i tell them the *rules*, and it makes it — *(she searches for the word, finds a kid's version of it)* …*smaller.* the *scared* gets *smaller* when somebody *tells* you the *rules.* …*(she nods at her own wisdom)* …*you* already *know* the *rules*, i bet. *old*-cold *always* does. …so what do *you* *want*, at *my* *step?*

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(She tells you about the Job with the grave pride of a child entrusted with something real, which is exactly what she has entrusted herself with.)* …*here's* how it *works.* the *new* dead come *up* the *queue* all *cold* and *lost*, and the *big* people *push* *past* them — everybody's got their *own* *dark* to *walk* into, nobody *stops* — and the *littlest* ones just …*stand* there. *shaking.* *nobody* *coming.* …*(her jaw sets)* …*i* *stand* there. i *stopped* *walking* into my *dark* a *long* time ago and i *stand* at the *step* *instead*, and when a *scared* one comes i *wave* them *over* and i *give* them the *rules* and i *hold* their *hand* till the *shaking* *stops.* …*(matter of fact)* …somebody *has* to. it's the *worst* place there *is* and it's *full* of *kids* nobody *came* for. …*(she looks at you)* …*that's* the *Job.* …i'm *good* at it. …i've been *good* at it …a *really* *long* *time.*

choices.text: *(read the child who gives everyone the greeting she never got — gently, because she is soft at the edges and does not know it)* "Can I look at you a second, Sparrow? Not to take you anywhere. Just to see the kid doing the biggest job in the grey."

*(the old-cold one, who knows things a kid at a step can't)* "I'll tell you what I am, Sparrow, because you've earned straight talk: I'm the one soul the Wall ever gave back. I walk in and out of the dark you all only walk into. Which means I can do things here nobody else can. So when you tell me what you're really afraid of — and it isn't the dark — I might actually be able to help."
"There's a new one over there, Sparrow. Small. Scared. Standing off the step. Want me to help you bring them in?"

*(she is going soft at the edges — say it kindly, and ask the real question)* "Sparrow. Your edges are going soft. You know what that means, don't you. What happens to the step — to the scared kids who come after — when you're not here to catch them?"
"Tell me a rule, Sparrow. Your favorite one. The one that helps most."

*(the thing no one ever did for her — sit down on the step, take her small soft hand, and greet the greeter)* "Sparrow. Come here a second. I want to tell you the rules. You can always sit on the step. Nobody sits alone. And it's okay that you're scared — because being scared means you had something good enough to be scared of losing. I'm greeting you, Sparrow. Somebody finally gets to greet you."

*(give her the one thing she actually needs — not rescue, but that the Job outlives her)* "Here's my promise, Sparrow, and I keep my promises: the step won't be empty. I'll find the next keeper. That little one you're about to greet will learn your rules, and grow into them, and sit this step when you're gone, and greet the one after that. The chain won't break. You won't be the last."
"Keep your post, Sparrow. I'll be back."

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(She holds still for you, proud to be looked at, and your sense moves past the brave competent lookout to the small soul underneath.)* *(And you find the Job is a gift she is giving the whole grey out of the exact shape of her own oldest wound. Your sense reads the arrival she has never told anyone: that she came here littlest and newest and most afraid, up the same queue, and stood at that same step shaking and calling and calling — and that no one came. No god, no greeter, no hand. The big dead pushed past. She stood alone in the worst place there is for what must have been, to a small soul, an eternity, until the shaking finally stopped on its own, which is a thing that should never have to happen to a child. And your sense reads what she built out of that: not bitterness, but a vow, wordless and total — that no other little one would ever stand at that step the way she did. That she would be, for every frightened child after her, the thing that did not come for her. And it reads the ache under the whole brave enterprise, the one she cannot let herself feel because feeling it would stop her working: that in all the long time she has spent making sure no child is alone, she has never once, herself, been greeted. She holds every shaking hand and no one has ever held hers. She invented the rule 'nobody sits alone' and is the loneliest soul on the step. And your sense reads the fear now sharpening as her edges soften: not of dissolving — she is oddly unafraid of that — but that when she goes, the step goes unwatched, and the next littlest one will come up the queue and stand there shaking and calling, and no one will come, exactly the way no one came for her, and her whole reason for staying will unravel with her.)*

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(She takes the straight talk the way kids take being treated as real — with a seriousness that breaks your heart a little.)* …in and *out* of the *dark.* …*(she considers this with genuine professional interest)* …that's — okay, that's *actually* *useful*, old-cold, most *grown* *magic* is *useless* here. …*(and then she gets to the real thing, and her brave voice wobbles for the first time)* …you *want* to know what i'm *scared* of. …*(she looks at her own hands, and maybe notices, for the first time, the softness at the edges)* …it's *not* the *dark.* i *stopped* being *scared* of *my* *dark* when i gave myself the *Job.* …*(quietly)* …it's that i'm — i think i'm going *soft.* the *going*-*away* soft. and when i *go* …*(her chin comes up, fierce and frightened at once)* …*who* *sits* on the *step?* …who *catches* the *next* little *one* when they come up the *queue* all *shaking* and *calling* and — *(she can't finish)* …*nobody* *caught* — …*(she stops herself, redirects, protective even now of her own wound)* …*somebody* has to *catch* them, old-cold. …*promise* me you're *thinking* about *who.*

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(She is up off the step before you've finished the sentence, all business, the softness in her edges forgotten in the face of a Job to do.)* …*where* — *(she sees the new one, and her whole face changes, goes gentle and sure)* …*oh.* *little.* *really* little. and *really* scared, look how *still* they're — *(she's already moving)* …*okay.* *okay*, here's what we *do*, old-cold, and you *help* me, because *two* is *less* *scary* than *one* coming at a *shaking* kid. …you *stay* *back* a *little*, and you make your *cold* go *quiet* — you *can* do that, *soften* it — and you let *me* go *first*, low and *slow*, and i say the *rules*, and you just — you just *be* a *warm* *quiet* *shape* *behind* me so they know the *world's* not *all* *sharp* *edges.* …*(she glances back at you, fierce and pleading)* …*don't* *scare* them. …they've been *scared* *enough* for *one* *death.* …*come* *on.* *gently.* …*greeting* time.

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(You name it plainly, and she doesn't pretend, which tells you how long she's been carrying it alone.)* …*(she looks at her softening hands, and for once doesn't hide them)* …*yeah.* …i *know.* …*(a small, brave, terrible breath)* …i'm not — it's *funny*, everybody thinks the *scary* part is the *going*-*away.* it's *not.* i'm — i'm almost — *(she can't quite say 'ready')* …the scary part is *after.* …*(she gestures at the step, the queue, the whole cold machine of it)* …a *new* little one is going to come up that *queue* — *tomorrow*, or in an *hour*, time's *broken* here — all *cold* and *calling* — and the *step's* going to be *empty.* …and they're going to *stand* there. *shaking.* *nobody* coming. …*(her voice cracks, finally)* …*just* *like* — …*(she stops. she has never said the next word to anyone.)* …*(barely)* …*just* like *i* *did.* …*nobody* *came* for *me*, old-cold. that's — that's the *whole* *reason* there's a *Job.* i made *sure* no one would *ever* *stand* there like *i* — and now i'm *going*, and it'll *happen* *again*, and i *can't* — *(she wipes her face with the heel of her hand, furious at the tears)* …*fix* it. *please.* …i don't *care* what happens to *me.* …just don't let the *step* be *empty.*

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(She brightens instantly — asking a kid about their system is the surest kindness there is — and rattles it off with total conviction.)* …*okay* so *rule* *one* is you can *always* sit on the *step*, i *said* that one. *rule* *two* is *nobody* sits *alone.* …but my *favorite* — the one that *helps* the *most* — is *rule* *five.* …*(she leans in, imparting the secret)* …*rule* *five* is: *'it's* *okay* that you're *scared.* being *scared* means you *had* something *good* enough to be *scared* of *losing.'* …*(proud)* …i made that one up for a boy who kept *saying* *sorry* for *crying.* i told him *crying's* just *love* with *nowhere* to *go*, and being *scared* is just — it's *proof* you *had* a *good* *life*, because you only get *this* *scared* about *leaving* something *warm.* …*(she nods, fierce)* …the *scared* ones always think being scared means they're *weak.* it *doesn't.* it means they *loved* their *life.* …*(quieter)* …that one *always* makes the *shaking* stop. …*every* time. …it's a *really* *good* *rule.*

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(You don't promise her a successor. Not yet. You do the other thing — the thing no one ever did — you sit down on the step beside her, and you make your cold go quiet and warm, and you take her small soft hand in yours, and you say the rules back to her. You tell her she can always sit on the step. You tell her nobody sits alone. You tell her it's okay that she's scared — that being scared means she had something good enough to be scared of losing. You greet her. The greeter, greeted, at last.)* …*(she goes very still)* …*(and then the brave competent lookout, the keeper of the step, the kid who has held a thousand shaking hands, is just a small child again, having her hand held for the first time since she died)* …*you're* — *(her voice is very small)* …*you're* *doing* the *Job.* …*on* *me.* …*(she starts to cry, and it's different from before, it's not the furious wiping-away kind, it's the kind that comes when you finally, finally get to set a thing down)* …*nobody* ever — …i *do* it for *everyone*, and *nobody* ever — …*(she leans against your side, and her softening edges seem, for this moment, to still)* …*rule* *five.* …you said *rule* *five* to *me.* …*(a wet, astonished laugh)* …it *works.* …it *actually* — the *shaking* *stopped.* …*(she looks up at you, and there is something settled in her face now, something a soul needs before it can let go)* …*okay.* …*okay*, old-cold. …i can — i think i can *go* now. …not because the *step* doesn't *matter.* …because *you* *know* about it *now.* …and a soul who'd *sit* *down* and *greet* the *greeter* …*(she squeezes your hand)* …*that's* a soul who *won't* *let* the *step* …be *empty.* …*right?* …*(the smallest, bravest, most trusting question there is)* …*right?*

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(You promise her the thing she actually needs — not that she'll be saved, but that the Job won't die with her. You tell her you'll find the next keeper of the step. That the new one she's about to greet — the small scared one off the step — will learn the rules, and grow into them, and take the post when Sparrow is soft-and-gone, and greet the one after that. That you'll make sure the chain doesn't break.)* …*(she listens with her whole body, and you watch a weight she's carried alone for an age begin, at last, to lift)* …*a* — a *next* one. …someone to *learn* the *rules* …and *sit* the *step* …*after.* …*(her eyes shine)* …*that's* — old-cold, *that's* the *only* thing i *ever* *wanted.* not to *stay.* just to *not* be the *last.* …*(fierce, hopeful)* …*teach* them *rule* *five.* *promise* me. the *scared*-*means*-*you*-*loved*-*your*-*life* one. it's the *best* one, it does the *most* *work.* …*(she nods, settling something enormous)* …*okay.* …then it's — then it's *okay* that i'm going *soft.* …because the *step* *won't* be *empty.* …*(she goes back to her post, lighter, watching the queue with her softening eyes)* …*(and then, quietly, almost to herself)* …*somebody's* *coming* to *catch* them. …*finally* …*somebody's* *coming.*

nodes.speaker: Sparrow
nodes.text: *(She nods, already turning back to the queue, legs swinging, eyes peeled for the next shaking arrival.)* …*'course.* *post* doesn't *keep* *itself.* …*(and as you go, she calls after, a kid's version of profound)* …hey — old-cold. …*you* were *scared* *once* too, i *bet.* everybody was. …*(matter of fact, kind)* …*rule* *five*, okay? …whatever it *was* you *lost* …you were *scared* about it because it was *good.* …*(she waves, and goes back to watching)* …*that* one's *free.* …i give the *first* one *free.*

conversations.title: the new one — a frightened child

nodes.speaker: the new one
nodes.text: *(Up close they are very small and very new, the cold of death still strange on them, and they look at you with the wide unguarded terror of a child who has not yet learned to hide anything.)* …*(a tiny voice)* …you're — you're not *sharp.* …*(surprised)* …the — the *big* ones going *past* are all *sharp* and *fast* and they don't — nobody *looks* at me. …but you *looked.* …*(they clutch the nightshirt hem tighter)* …i *died.* …*(they say it like they're testing whether it's true by saying it)* …i *think* i *died.* i was *sick* and then it was *cold* and then i was *here* and *nobody* — i *called* for my *mum* and she didn't — …*(the lip trembles)* …is she *coming?* …does she *come* *here* *too?* …*(and then, in a smaller voice, the real fear)* …*am* i going to be *here* …*by* *myself* …*forever?*

choices.text: *(read the smallest soul — the softest touch you have, barely a brush)* "Can I look at you, little one? Just for a second. I'll be gentle."

*(tell them what you are, in words a small scared kid can hold)* "I'm not the one who takes you. I'm the opposite, a little bit. I'm the one who went into the dark and came back out — the only one who does. So I'm not scary, even though I'm cold. Cold just means I've been where you are, and I know the way back to warm."

*(don't answer the big questions — point them at the one warm thing in sight)* "You're not going to be by yourself. See the girl on the step? Sparrow? She's been waving you over. She keeps the step so nobody has to be alone here. She's very good at it. Will you let me walk you to her?"

*(the truth, kindly — you can't promise their mum, but you can promise they won't be alone)* "I can't promise you your mum, little one. I won't lie to you — you've had enough people not tell you things. But I can promise you this: you will not be here by yourself. That much I can make true, right now."
"Stay right here a moment. Don't be afraid. I'm coming back."

nodes.speaker: the new one
nodes.text: *(They turn this over with the enormous seriousness small children give to the rules of a new place.)* …you went *in* …and came *back* *out.* …*(their eyes go wide)* …so the *dark* isn't — the dark isn't a *forever* thing? …you can *come* *back*? …*(and then, the leap a child makes)* …can *i*? …can i come *back* *out*, like you? …*(and you have to find the gentle true answer — that most souls don't, that the dark is long, but that they will not walk into it alone and not today and not while there's a step to sit on)* …*(they listen, and something in the wide terror softens toward trust)* …*okay.* …you *know* about the *dark* …and you're not *scared* of it. …*(a tiny breath)* …then i'll — i'll be a *little* less *scared* of it *too.* …because *you* did it …and you're *still* *kind.* …*(a whisper)* …the *dark* didn't make you *sharp.* …*(that seems to reassure them most of all)*

nodes.speaker: the new one
nodes.text: *(You reach, barely, the softest brush of sense, and find exactly what you feared and hoped: a whole child, entire, undissolved, brand new to the dark.)* *(And your sense finds no great secret, because they are too young and too new to have built one — just a small soul made almost entirely of the life they just left, still warm with it, still shaped like a kitchen and a mother's voice and a favorite blanket and being sick and being frightened and not understanding. And your sense reads the one thing they need, and it is the simplest thing there is, the thing every frightened new soul needs and so few get: not an explanation of death, which they couldn't hold, not a promise about their mother, which no one can keep — just a warm shape that stays. Presence. A hand. The animal comfort of not being alone in the dark. And your sense reads, with a pang, that this — this exact need, in this exact small soul — is the thing Sparrow built her whole afterlife to answer, and that the greatest kindness you could do here is not to be the one who answers it, but to carry this little one the ten steps to the child who has been waiting her whole death to be allowed to.)*

nodes.speaker: the new one
nodes.text: *(They look toward the step, toward Sparrow, who has gone still and gentle and is patting the stone beside her, and you feel the small hand tighten on yours and then, bravely, loosen.)* …*(a whisper)* …she's — she's *little.* like *me.* …*(that seems to matter more than anything a grown soul could have said)* …the *big* ones are all *sharp* …but *she's* — …*(a very small, very brave step toward the step)* …*okay.* …*okay*, i'll — will you *come* *too?* just — just *partway?* …*(and you walk them in, slow and quiet, a warm shape behind, and Sparrow looks up and says 'hi, i'm Sparrow, you can always sit on the step, that's rule one,' and you watch the smallest soul in the grey stop shaking, because a kid told them the rules)* …*(they sit. they don't let go of Sparrow's hand. Sparrow doesn't let go either.)* …*(from the step, the new one looks back at you, and the terror is gone, replaced by the plain trust of a child who has been handed to safety)* …*thank* you. …*for* the — for the *warm* *shape.* …*(they turn back to Sparrow, who is already saying rule two)*

nodes.speaker: the new one
nodes.text: *(They take it — the hard honesty and the warm promise together — the way children take the truth when it's finally, respectfully told: better than the lie.)* …*(a shaky breath)* …you're not — you're not *pretending.* …*(they seem almost relieved)* …*everybody* *pretends* to *little* kids. the *nurse* *pretended* i was getting *better.* …*(quietly)* …you *said* the *true* thing. …and the *true* thing has a — a *warm* part *in* it. *'you* *won't* be *by* *yourself.'* …*(they hold onto that)* …*okay.* …i can — i can do the *not*-*by*-*myself* part. …*(they look at you, steadier)* …the *by*-*myself*-*forever* was the — that was the *worst* one. …worse than the *cold.* worse than *dying.* …*(a tiny, brave nod)* …if it's not *that* one …then i think …i think i can be *okay.* …*will* you *stay* …till the *not*-*alone* *starts?*

nodes.speaker: the new one
nodes.text: *(The question steadies them — a name is a rope back to being a person.)* …*(a sniff)* …*Eli.* …*(they say it carefully, like it's precious, which it is)* …*mum* calls me — *called* me — *'Eli-bean.'* …*(the memory both hurts and helps)* …*(and then, looking at you with those wide new-dead eyes)* …is my *name* still — do i still *get* to *keep* it? here? …*(a whisper)* …the *cold* feels like it wants to — like it's *reaching* for the *edges* of me. …*(frightened)* …i don't want to *forget* being *Eli.* …*(they clutch the nightshirt)* …*tell* me i get to *keep* being *Eli.* …*please.* …*(the smallest, most important question a soul can ask)* …*please* tell me i get to *stay* *me.*

nodes.speaker: the new one
nodes.text: *(They nod fast, and try very hard to be brave, and mostly manage it because you asked them to.)* …*okay.* …*(a wobbly breath)* …i'll — i'll *stay* right *here.* i won't *move.* …*(and as you turn, in a very small voice)* …*you're* *coming* *back?* …*(they need to hear it twice)* …you *said* you're *coming* *back.* …*(they hold onto the words like the hem of the nightshirt)* …*people* *say* that. …the *nurse* *said* that. …*(barely)* …but *you* *looked* at me. …so *maybe* …*maybe* you're *one* of the *ones* …who *comes* *back.*
